mnemonics.title: Support Vectors Are Vital
mnemonics.content: Only the points closest to the hyperplane (support vectors) matter for defining the decision boundary. Remember: SVAV - Support Vectors Are Vital

mnemonics.title: Maximum Margin Magic
mnemonics.content: SVM finds the hyperplane with the maximum margin. Remember: MAMM - Making A Maximum Margin

mnemonics.title: Kernel Kindly Keeps Knowledge
mnemonics.content: Kernel functions transform data to higher dimensions to make it linearly separable. Remember: KKK - Kernel Kindly Keeps Knowledge

mnemonics.title: C Controls Complexity
mnemonics.content: The C parameter controls the tradeoff between maximizing margin and minimizing classification errors. High C = strict boundary, Low C = wider margin

mnemonics.title: Gamma Guides Globes
mnemonics.content: Gamma parameter defines how far the influence of a single training example reaches. High gamma = small influence radius (complex model), Low gamma = large influence (simple model)

acronyms.usage: The main classification algorithm - used for both linear and non-linear separation
Most common kernel - creates Gaussian bells around data points. Used when data has complex, non-linear patterns
Optimality conditions used in SVM optimization - tells us which points become support vectors
Allows some misclassifications in soft margin SVM - helps handle noisy data
Used to penalize misclassified points - max(0, 1 - y*f(x))

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a straight line (2D) or flat plane (3D) that separates two classes like a divider on a road. It's the decision boundary!
Picture fence posts standing on the boundary line - these are the critical data points that 'hold up' the decision boundary
Think of a wide street with the hyperplane as the center line. The margin is the width of the street - SVM wants the widest street possible
Imagine lifting flat 2D data points with springs into 3D space - points that couldn't be separated on paper become separable when lifted
Linear = drawing a straight line to separate classes. Non-linear = drawing curved boundaries or using kernel transformation
Hard margin = strict teacher allowing zero mistakes. Soft margin = friendly teacher allowing some mistakes for better overall separation

analogies.analogy: A referee line in a sports game that separates two teams - players on either side belong to different teams
The most critical students in a class who sit exactly on the passing grade boundary - remove them and the boundary changes
The safety cushion in parallel parking - the wider the margin, the more confident you are about not hitting cars
Unfolding a crumpled paper to separate dots that were overlapping when flat - you add a dimension (height) to solve separation
A strict parent (high C) demands perfect behavior = strict boundary. A lenient parent (low C) allows some mistakes = wider margin
The reach of your influence. High gamma = you're popular only with close friends (local). Low gamma = you're known by everyone (global)
